main_ingredients: mixed vegetables (cauliflower, carrot, green peas, potato, capsicum, beans)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Veg Kolhapuri is approximately 250 g, typically served as a main curry dish alongside rice or Indian bread.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Mixed Vegetable Curry (Sabzi) composite reference
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No specific ICMR-NIN entry for Veg Kolhapuri exists. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN mixed vegetable curry (sabzi) reference values, adjusted for the higher fat and spice content characteristic of Kolhapuri-style preparations. Kolhapuri masala includes coconut, sesame, and poppy seeds which elevate fat and energy relative to a plain vegetable curry. Values represent a best-estimate composite consistent with ICMR-NIN methodology.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed vegetable curry analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Veg Kolhapuri. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous mixed vegetable curry entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is dominated by non-starchy vegetables, legumes, and a spiced coconut-based masala, all of which are associated with low GI values. Potato content is modest and offset by high fibre, fat, and protein content which slow gastric emptying. GL per serving calculated as (45 × 11.0 × 2.50) / 100 = 12.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 320 mg per 100 g; a 250 g serving delivers approximately 800 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size as a single serving contributes approximately 35% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit; requesting reduced-salt preparation or limiting accompaniments such as pickles and papadums is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) driven by high dietary fibre (3.0 g/100 g), fat, and protein content that slow glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Veg Kolhapuri has a low GI of approximately 45 and a moderate glycemic load per serving (~12.4), making it a suitable curry option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed with low-GI bread or rice and within overall carbohydrate targets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol-free, plant-based dish with moderate total fat (6.5 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g); rich in dietary fibre and antioxidant spices
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Veg Kolhapuri is a cholesterol-free, fibre-rich vegetable curry; its moderate saturated fat content from coconut in the masala should be noted, but overall the dish supports a heart-healthy dietary pattern when consumed in standard portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veg Kolhapuri (also spelled Veg Kolapuri) is a fiery, deeply spiced mixed vegetable curry originating from the Kolhapur region of Maharashtra, western India. The dish is defined by its signature Kolhapuri masala, a complex spice blend that typically includes dried red Kashmiri and Bedgi chillies, roasted coconut, sesame seeds, poppy seeds, coriander, cumin, cloves, cinnamon, and black pepper, which are dry-roasted and ground to a paste. Common vegetables include cauliflower, carrot, green peas, potato, capsicum, and French beans, which are sautéed and then simmered in a rich, dark-red onion-tomato-masala gravy. The flavour profile is intensely hot, earthy, and aromatic with a slightly nutty undertone from the coconut and sesame. The texture is semi-dry to medium-thick. Nutritionally, Veg Kolhapuri provides approximately 112 kcal per 100 g, with 3.2 g protein, 6.5 g fat, and 11.0 g carbohydrate. It is a good source of dietary fibre (3.0 g/100 g), vitamin C, vitamin A, and iron. The dish is entirely plant-based, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Its low glycemic index (~45) makes it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is moderate to high (~320 mg/100 g) and should be monitored by those with hypertension. In Singapore, Veg Kolhapuri is served in Indian restaurants as a main curry dish, typically accompanied by naan, roti, or steamed rice.

confidence_reason: No exact ICMR-NIN or HPB entry exists for Veg Kolhapuri. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN mixed vegetable curry (sabzi) reference data with upward adjustment for fat and energy to reflect the coconut, sesame, and poppy seed content of Kolhapuri masala. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable mixed vegetable curry entries. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation methodology is transparent, the source organisation is primary-tier, and the macronutrient profile is internally consistent.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN mixed vegetable curry reference; no exact Veg Kolhapuri entry was found in any approved primary source. 2. GI value of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Veg Kolhapuri was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 3. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a fully plant-based dish containing no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin_b12 is naturally zero in plant foods. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this dish contains no animal-derived or fortified ingredients; vitamin_d is naturally zero in this plant-based preparation. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as no hydrogenated fats are used; naturally zero in this traditionally prepared dish. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a fully plant-based dish; cholesterol is naturally zero. 8. The food_name canonical form is 'Veg Kolhapuri' (standard spelling); 'Veg Kolapuri' is preserved as the primary alias reflecting the field survey data source spelling. 9. Multilingual names provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements, as this dish is served in Singapore. 10. FoodOn FOODON_00002809 and FoodEx2 A0CKP are used as closest_parent anchors; no exact ontology codes exist for this specific dish.
